verses.esv: Now the word of the LORD came to Jonah the son of Amittai, saying,
verses.kjv: NOW the word of the Lord came unto Jonah the son of Amittai, saying,
verses.bsb: Now the word of the LORD came to Jonah son of Amittai, saying,

verses.esv: “Arise, go to Nineveh, that great city, and call out against it, for their evil has come up before me.”
verses.kjv: Arise, go to Nineveh, that great city, and cry against it; for their wickedness is come up before me.
verses.bsb: “Get up! Go to the great city of Nineveh and preach against it, because its wickedness has come up before Me.”

verses.esv: But Jonah rose to flee to Tarshish from the presence of the LORD. He went down to Joppa and found a ship going to Tarshish. So he paid the fare and went down into it, to go with them to Tarshish, away from the presence of the LORD.
verses.kjv: But Jonah rose up to flee unto Tarshish from the presence of the Lord, and went down to Joppa; and he found a ship going to Tarshish: so he paid the fare thereof, and went down into it, to go with them unto Tarshish from the presence of the Lord.
verses.bsb: Jonah, however, got up to flee to Tarshish, away from the presence of the LORD. He went down to Joppa and found a ship bound for Tarshish. So he paid the fare and went aboard to sail for Tarshish, away from the presence of the LORD.

verses.esv: But the LORD hurled a great wind upon the sea, and there was a mighty tempest on the sea, so that the ship threatened to break up.
verses.kjv: ¶ But the Lord sent out a great wind into the sea, and there was a mighty tempest in the sea, so that the ship was like to be broken.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD hurled a great wind upon the sea, and such a violent storm arose that the ship was in danger of breaking apart.

verses.esv: Then the mariners were afraid, and each cried out to his god. And they hurled the cargo that was in the ship into the sea to lighten it for them. But Jonah had gone down into the inner part of the ship and had lain down and was fast asleep.
verses.kjv: Then the mariners were afraid, and cried every man unto his god, and cast forth the wares that were in the ship into the sea, to lighten it of them. But Jonah was gone down into the sides of the ship; and he lay, and was fast asleep.
verses.bsb: The sailors were afraid, and each cried out to his own god. And they threw the ship’s cargo into the sea to lighten the load. But Jonah had gone down to the lowest part of the vessel, where he lay down and fell into a deep sleep.

verses.esv: So the captain came and said to him, “What do you mean, you sleeper? Arise, call out to your god! Perhaps the god will give a thought to us, that we may not perish.”
verses.kjv: So the shipmaster came to him, and said unto him, What meanest thou, O sleeper? arise, call upon thy God, if so be that God will think upon us, that we perish not.
verses.bsb: The captain approached him and said, “How can you sleep? Get up and call upon your God. Perhaps this God will consider us, so that we may not perish.”

verses.esv: And they said to one another, “Come, let us cast lots, that we may know on whose account this evil has come upon us.” So they cast lots, and the lot fell on Jonah.
verses.kjv: And they said every one to his fellow, Come, and let us cast lots, that we may know for whose cause this evil is upon us. So they cast lots, and the lot fell upon Jonah.
verses.bsb: “Come!” said the sailors to one another. “Let us cast lots to find out who is responsible for this calamity that is upon us.”  So they cast lots, and the lot fell on Jonah.

verses.esv: Then they said to him, “Tell us on whose account this evil has come upon us. What is your occupation? And where do you come from? What is your country? And of what people are you?”
verses.kjv: Then said they unto him, Tell us, we pray thee, for whose cause this evil is upon us; What is thine occupation? and whence comest thou? what is thy country? and of what people art thou?
verses.bsb: “Tell us now,” they demanded, “who is to blame for this calamity that is upon us? What is your occupation, and where have you come from? What is your country, and who are your people?”

verses.esv: And he said to them, “I am a Hebrew, and I fear the LORD, the God of heaven, who made the sea and the dry land.”
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, I am an Hebrew; and I fear the Lord, the God of heaven, which hath made the sea and the dry land.
verses.bsb: “I am a Hebrew,” replied Jonah. “I worship the LORD, the God of the heavens, who made the sea and the dry land.”

verses.esv: Then the men were exceedingly afraid and said to him, “What is this that you have done!” For the men knew that he was fleeing from the presence of the LORD, because he had told them.
verses.kjv: Then were the men exceedingly afraid, and said unto him, Why hast thou done this? For the men knew that he fled from the presence of the Lord, because he had told them.
verses.bsb: Then the men were even more afraid and said to him, “What have you done?” The men knew that he was fleeing from the presence of the LORD, because he had told them.

verses.esv: Then they said to him, “What shall we do to you, that the sea may quiet down for us?” For the sea grew more and more tempestuous.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then said they unto him, What shall we do unto thee, that the sea may be calm unto us? for the sea wrought, and was tempestuous.
verses.bsb: Now the sea was growing worse and worse, so they said to Jonah, “What must we do to you to calm this sea for us?”

verses.esv: He said to them, “Pick me up and hurl me into the sea; then the sea will quiet down for you, for I know it is because of me that this great tempest has come upon you.”
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Take me up, and cast me forth into the sea; so shall the sea be calm unto you: for I know that for my sake this great tempest is upon you.
verses.bsb: “Pick me up,” he answered, “and cast me into the sea, so it may quiet down for you. For I know that I am to blame for this violent storm that has come upon you.”

verses.esv: Nevertheless, the men rowed hard to get back to dry land, but they could not, for the sea grew more and more tempestuous against them.
verses.kjv: Nevertheless the men rowed hard to bring it to the land; but they could not: for the sea wrought, and was tempestuous against them.
verses.bsb: Nevertheless, the men rowed hard to get back to dry land, but they could not, for the sea was raging against them more and more.

verses.esv: Therefore they called out to the LORD, “O LORD, let us not perish for this man's life, and lay not on us innocent blood, for you, O LORD, have done as it pleased you.”
verses.kjv: Wherefore they cried unto the Lord, and said, We beseech thee, O Lord, we beseech thee, let us not perish for this man’s life, and lay not upon us innocent blood: for thou, O Lord, hast done as it pleased thee.
verses.bsb: So they cried out to the LORD: “Please, O LORD, do not let us perish on account of this man’s life! Do not charge us with innocent blood! For You, O LORD, have done as You pleased.”

verses.esv: So they picked up Jonah and hurled him into the sea, and the sea ceased from its raging.
verses.kjv: So they took up Jonah, and cast him forth into the sea: and the sea ceased from her raging.
verses.bsb: At this, they picked up Jonah and cast him into the sea, and the raging sea grew calm.

verses.esv: Then the men feared the LORD exceedingly, and they offered a sacrifice to the LORD and made vows.
verses.kjv: Then the men feared the Lord exceedingly, and offered a sacrifice unto the Lord, and made vows.
verses.bsb: Then the men feared the LORD greatly, and they offered a sacrifice to the LORD and made vows to Him.

verses.esv: And the LORD appointed a great fish to swallow up Jonah. And Jonah was in the belly of the fish three days and three nights.
verses.kjv: ¶ Now the Lord had prepared a great fish to swallow up Jonah. And Jonah was in the belly of the fish three days and three nights.
verses.bsb: Now the LORD had appointed a great fish to swallow Jonah, and Jonah spent three days and three nights in the belly of the fish.

verses.esv: Then Jonah prayed to the LORD his God from the belly of the fish,
verses.kjv: THEN Jonah prayed unto the Lord his God out of the fish’s belly,
verses.bsb: From inside the fish, Jonah prayed to the LORD his God,

verses.esv: saying, “I called out to the LORD, out of my distress, and he answered me; out of the belly of Sheol I cried, and you heard my voice.
verses.kjv: And said, I cried by reason of mine affliction unto the Lord, and he heard me; out of the belly of hell cried I, and thou heardest my voice.
verses.bsb: saying:  “In my distress I called to the LORD, and He answered me. From the belly of Sheol I called for help, and You heard my voice.

verses.esv: For you cast me into the deep, into the heart of the seas, and the flood surrounded me; all your waves and your billows passed over me.
verses.kjv: For thou hadst cast me into the deep, in the midst of the seas; and the floods compassed me about: all thy billows and thy waves passed over me.
verses.bsb: For You cast me into the deep, into the heart of the seas, and the current swirled about me; all Your breakers and waves swept over me.

verses.esv: Then I said, ‘I am driven away from your sight; yet I shall again look upon your holy temple.’
verses.kjv: Then I said, I am cast out of thy sight; yet I will look again toward thy holy temple.
verses.bsb: At this, I said, ‘I have been banished from Your sight; yet I will look once more toward Your holy temple.’

verses.esv: The waters closed in over me to take my life; the deep surrounded me; weeds were wrapped about my head
verses.kjv: The waters compassed me about, even to the soul: the depth closed me round about, the weeds were wrapped about my head.
verses.bsb: The waters engulfed me to take my life; the watery depths closed around me; the seaweed wrapped around my head.

verses.esv: at the roots of the mountains. I went down to the land whose bars closed upon me forever; yet you brought up my life from the pit, O LORD my God.
verses.kjv: I went down to the bottoms of the mountains; the earth with her bars was about me for ever: yet hast thou brought up my life from corruption, O Lord my God.
verses.bsb: To the roots of the mountains I descended; the earth beneath me barred me in forever! But You raised my life from the pit, O LORD my God!

verses.esv: When my life was fainting away, I remembered the LORD, and my prayer came to you, into your holy temple.
verses.kjv: When my soul fainted within me I remembered the Lord: and my prayer came in unto thee, into thine holy temple.
verses.bsb: As my life was fading away, I remembered the LORD. My prayer went up to You, to Your holy temple.

verses.esv: Those who pay regard to vain idols forsake their hope of steadfast love.
verses.kjv: They that observe lying vanities forsake their own mercy.
verses.bsb: Those who cling to worthless idols forsake His loving devotion.

verses.esv: But I with the voice of thanksgiving will sacrifice to you; what I have vowed I will pay. Salvation belongs to the LORD!”
verses.kjv: But I will sacrifice unto thee with the voice of thanksgiving; I will pay that that I have vowed. Salvation is of the Lord.
verses.bsb: But I, with the voice of thanksgiving, will sacrifice to You. I will fulfill what I have vowed. Salvation is from the LORD!”

verses.esv: And the LORD spoke to the fish, and it vomited Jonah out upon the dry land.
verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord spake unto the fish, and it vomited out Jonah upon the dry land.
verses.bsb: And the LORD commanded the fish, and it vomited Jonah onto dry land.

verses.esv: Then the word of the LORD came to Jonah the second time, saying,
verses.kjv: AND the word of the Lord came unto Jonah the second time, saying,
verses.bsb: Then the word of the LORD came to Jonah a second time:

verses.esv: “Arise, go to Nineveh, that great city, and call out against it the message that I tell you.”
verses.kjv: Arise, go unto Nineveh, that great city, and preach unto it the preaching that I bid thee.
verses.bsb: “Get up! Go to the great city of Nineveh and proclaim to it the message that I give you.”

verses.esv: So Jonah arose and went to Nineveh, according to the word of the LORD. Now Nineveh was an exceedingly great city, three days' journey in breadth.
verses.kjv: So Jonah arose, and went unto Nineveh, according to the word of the Lord. Now Nineveh was an exceeding great city of three days’ journey.
verses.bsb: This time Jonah got up and went to Nineveh, in accordance with the word of the LORD.  Now Nineveh was an exceedingly great city, requiring a three-day journey.

verses.esv: Jonah began to go into the city, going a day's journey. And he called out, “Yet forty days, and Nineveh shall be overthrown!”
verses.kjv: And Jonah began to enter into the city a day’s journey, and he cried, and said, Yet forty days, and Nineveh shall be overthrown.
verses.bsb: On the first day of his journey, Jonah set out into the city and proclaimed, “Forty more days and Nineveh will be overturned!”

verses.esv: And the people of Nineveh believed God. They called for a fast and put on sackcloth, from the greatest of them to the least of them.
verses.kjv: ¶ So the people of Nineveh believed God, and proclaimed a fast, and put on sackcloth, from the greatest of them even to the least of them.
verses.bsb: And the Ninevites believed God. They proclaimed a fast and dressed in sackcloth, from the greatest of them to the least.

verses.esv: The word reached the king of Nineveh, and he arose from his throne, removed his robe, covered himself with sackcloth, and sat in ashes.
verses.kjv: For word came unto the king of Nineveh, and he arose from his throne, and he laid his robe from him, and covered him with sackcloth, and sat in ashes.
verses.bsb: When word reached the king of Nineveh, he got up from his throne, took off his royal robe, covered himself with sackcloth, and sat in ashes.

verses.esv: And he issued a proclamation and published through Nineveh, “By the decree of the king and his nobles: Let neither man nor beast, herd nor flock, taste anything. Let them not feed or drink water,
verses.kjv: And he caused it to be proclaimed and published through Nineveh by the decree of the king and his nobles, saying, Let neither man nor beast, herd nor flock, taste any thing: let them not feed, nor drink water:
verses.bsb: Then he issued a proclamation in Nineveh:  “By the decree of the king and his nobles:  Let no man or beast, herd or flock, taste anything at all. They must not eat or drink.

verses.esv: but let man and beast be covered with sackcloth, and let them call out mightily to God. Let everyone turn from his evil way and from the violence that is in his hands.
verses.kjv: But let man and beast be covered with sackcloth, and cry mightily unto God: yea, let them turn every one from his evil way, and from the violence that is in their hands.
verses.bsb: Furthermore, let both man and beast be covered with sackcloth, and have everyone call out earnestly to God. Let each one turn from his evil ways and from the violence in his hands.

verses.esv: Who knows? God may turn and relent and turn from his fierce anger, so that we may not perish.”
verses.kjv: Who can tell if God will turn and repent, and turn away from his fierce anger, that we perish not?
verses.bsb: Who knows? God may turn and relent; He may turn from His fierce anger, so that we will not perish.”

verses.esv: When God saw what they did, how they turned from their evil way, God relented of the disaster that he had said he would do to them, and he did not do it.
verses.kjv: ¶ And God saw their works, that they turned from their evil way; and God repented of the evil, that he had said that he would do unto them; and he did it not.
verses.bsb: When God saw their actions—that they had turned from their evil ways—He relented from the disaster He had threatened to bring upon them.

verses.esv: But it displeased Jonah exceedingly, and he was angry.
verses.kjv: BUT it displeased Jonah exceedingly, and he was very angry.
verses.bsb: Jonah, however, was greatly displeased, and he became angry.

verses.esv: And he prayed to the LORD and said, “O LORD, is not this what I said when I was yet in my country? That is why I made haste to flee to Tarshish; for I knew that you are a gracious God and merciful, slow to anger and abounding in steadfast love, and relenting from disaster.
verses.kjv: And he prayed unto the Lord, and said, I pray thee, O Lord, was not this my saying, when I was yet in my country? Therefore I fled before unto Tarshish: for I knew that thou art a gracious God, and merciful, slow to anger, and of great kindness, and repentest thee of the evil.
verses.bsb: So he prayed to the LORD, saying, “O LORD, is this not what I said while I was still in my own country? This is why I was so quick to flee toward Tarshish. I knew that You are a gracious and compassionate God, slow to anger, abounding in loving devotion—One who relents from sending disaster.

verses.esv: Therefore now, O LORD, please take my life from me, for it is better for me to die than to live.”
verses.kjv: Therefore now, O Lord, take, I beseech thee, my life from me; for it is better for me to die than to live.
verses.bsb: And now, O LORD, please take my life from me, for it is better for me to die than to live.”

verses.esv: And the LORD said, “Do you do well to be angry?”
verses.kjv: ¶ Then said the Lord, Doest thou well to be angry?
verses.bsb: But the LORD replied, “Have you any right to be angry?”

verses.esv: Jonah went out of the city and sat to the east of the city and made a booth for himself there. He sat under it in the shade, till he should see what would become of the city.
verses.kjv: So Jonah went out of the city, and sat on the east side of the city, and there made him a booth, and sat under it in the shadow, till he might see what would become of the city.
verses.bsb: Then Jonah left the city and sat down east of it, where he made himself a shelter and sat in its shade to see what would happen to the city.

verses.esv: Now the LORD God appointed a plant and made it come up over Jonah, that it might be a shade over his head, to save him from his discomfort. So Jonah was exceedingly glad because of the plant.
verses.kjv: And the Lord God prepared a gourd, and made it to come up over Jonah, that it might be a shadow over his head, to deliver him from his grief. So Jonah was exceeding glad of the gourd.
verses.bsb: So the LORD God appointed a vine, and it grew up to provide shade over Jonah’s head to ease his discomfort, and Jonah was greatly pleased with the plant.

verses.esv: But when dawn came up the next day, God appointed a worm that attacked the plant, so that it withered.
verses.kjv: But God prepared a worm when the morning rose the next day, and it smote the gourd that it withered.
verses.bsb: When dawn came the next day, God appointed a worm that attacked the plant so that it withered.

verses.esv: When the sun rose, God appointed a scorching east wind, and the sun beat down on the head of Jonah so that he was faint. And he asked that he might die and said, “It is better for me to die than to live.”
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, when the sun did arise, that God prepared a vehement east wind; and the sun beat upon the head of Jonah, that he fainted, and wished in himself to die, and said, It is better for me to die than to live.
verses.bsb: As the sun was rising, God appointed a scorching east wind, and the sun beat down on Jonah’s head so that he grew faint and wished to die, saying, “It is better for me to die than to live.”

verses.esv: But God said to Jonah, “Do you do well to be angry for the plant?” And he said, “Yes, I do well to be angry, angry enough to die.”
verses.kjv: And God said to Jonah, Doest thou well to be angry for the gourd? And he said, I do well to be angry, even unto death.
verses.bsb: Then God asked Jonah, “Have you any right to be angry about the plant?”  “I do,” he replied. “I am angry enough to die!”

verses.esv: And the LORD said, “You pity the plant, for which you did not labor, nor did you make it grow, which came into being in a night and perished in a night.
verses.kjv: Then said the Lord, Thou hast had pity on the gourd, for the which thou hast not laboured, neither madest it grow; which came up in a night, and perished in a night:
verses.bsb: But the LORD said, “You cared about the plant, which you neither tended nor made grow. It sprang up in a night and perished in a night.

verses.esv: And should not I pity Nineveh, that great city, in which there are more than 120,000 persons who do not know their right hand from their left, and also much cattle?”
verses.kjv: And should not I spare Nineveh, that great city, wherein are more than sixscore thousand persons that cannot discern between their right hand and their left hand; and also much cattle?
verses.bsb: So should I not care about the great city of Nineveh, which has more than 120,000 people who cannot tell their right hand from their left, and many cattle as well?”
